description.en: Invented in 1982 by a Veneto miller out to out-French the baguette, ciabatta trades neat crumb for a wet, wildly open one. Home versions today push hydration past 80%, giving a thin, crackling crust that is perfect for panini and mopping up sauce.
description.de: 1982 von einem Müller aus Venetien erfunden, der dem Baguette Konkurrenz machen wollte: Die Ciabatta tauscht feine Krume gegen eine nasse, wild offenporige. Heutige Rezepte treiben die Hydration über 80 %, dünne, knusprige Kruste, ideal für Panini.
description.es: Inventada en 1982 por un molinero del Véneto que quería hacerle sombra a la baguette, la ciabatta cambia miga prieta por una húmeda y llena de alvéolos irregulares. Las versiones actuales superan el 80% de hidratación: corteza fina y crujiente, ideal para bocadillos.
description.fr: Inventée en 1982 par un meunier de Vénétie bien décidé à défier la baguette, la ciabatta troque une mie régulière contre une mie humide aux alvéoles sauvages. Les recettes actuelles dépassent 80% d'hydratation, croûte fine et craquante, parfaite en panini.

origin.note: Trademarked in 1982 by baker-miller Arnaldo Cavallari as 'Ciabatta Italia', created to defend Italian sandwich bread against the encroaching French baguette.

ingredients.note: 2 g in the poolish, 5 g in the final dough

keeping.note: Best the day it's baked; the thin crust turns chewy fast in humid air, but sliced loaves freeze and toast back to life.

method.title: Poolish
method.summary: The evening before, whisk equal parts flour and water with a little yeast into a loose batter and let it bubble at room temperature for 12-16 hours until domed and honeycombed.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Stir the poolish into the remaining flour and water; the result looks more like a thick batter than a bread dough. Work in the salt after a short rest.

method.title: Fold, don't knead
method.summary: Skip kneading. Give the wet dough 3-4 sets of gentle stretch-and-folds spaced 30 minutes apart over about 2-3 hours to build structure without deflating it.

method.title: Divide
method.summary: Tip the dough onto a heavily floured surface, cut into rectangles without pressing out the gas, and let the slipper shapes rest uncovered for roughly an hour.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Slide onto a hot stone at 250°C, spraying the oven walls for the first ten minutes to build steam, until the crust turns deep gold and thin, 20-25 minutes total.

source.adaptation: Formula follows this site's poolish method at 80% hydration; the original 1982 Cavallari trademark recipe used a stiffer 70% biga.
